user.title: Live Data Source
user.description:
Where should Solar AI get the live grid power, PV production, house load, and EV charging state from?

• **EVCC** — everything from EVCC (default)
• **Hybrid** — FoxESS for grid/PV/load, EVCC for EV info
• **FoxESS only** — no EVCC at all (EV charging is still detected via FoxESS Modbus TCP or the OCPP embedded server — see the warning on the next step)

evcc_url.title: EVCC Connection
evcc_url.description: Enter the URL of your EVCC instance.

data_description.evcc_url: Full URL including port, e.g. http://your-ha-ip:7070

quick.title: Quick setup

data.create_dashboard: Create the Solar AI dashboard for me

foxess_live_warning.title: ⚠️ EV coordination depends on your charger backend
foxess_live_warning.description:
You have selected **FoxESS only** as the live data source — Solar AI won't use EVCC.

EV charging is still detected automatically if you use either **FoxESS Modbus TCP** or **OCPP (embedded server)** as the charger backend — both read the charger directly, without needing EVCC.

If you use OCPP through a separate/external integration instead of the embedded server, Solar AI has no way to detect EV charging in this mode.

If the battery is grid-charging at the same time an undetected EV is drawing power, the combined load can exceed your main breaker. Only proceed if:
• You do NOT have an EV, OR
• Your EV charger is configured to never draw from the house battery, OR
• You're using one of the two directly-detected charger backends above

You must acknowledge this risk to continue.

data.acknowledge_no_ev: I have no EV, or my EV charger is configured to avoid the house battery

foxess_live_entities.title: FoxESS Live Sensors
foxess_live_entities.description: Pick the FoxESS Modbus sensors Solar AI should read live grid / PV / load from. Defaults are auto-detected.

foxess.title: Inverter Control Entities
foxess.description: Select the entities Solar AI uses to control the inverter. Auto-detected values are pre-filled — change them if your entity IDs differ.

data_description.foxess_inverter_id: The FoxESS Modbus inverter ID (a hex string). Used for direct register writes.
data_description.foxess_work_mode_entity: Select entity used to set the inverter work mode (Self Use / Feed-in First / Force Charge)
data_description.foxess_force_charge_entity: Number entity for setting the force-charge power (W)
data_description.foxess_force_discharge_entity: Number entity for setting the force-discharge / export power (W)

battery_sensors.title: Battery Sensor Entities
battery_sensors.description: Select the sensors Solar AI reads to monitor the battery. Auto-detected FoxESS Modbus values are pre-filled.

data_description.battery_soc_entity: Sensor reporting current SoC in percent (0–100)
data_description.cell_temp_entity: Sensor reporting the lowest cell temperature in °C — used for temperature-adaptive charge rates
data_description.battery_charge_entity: Sensor reporting current charge power in kW
data_description.battery_discharge_entity: Sensor reporting current discharge power in kW
data_description.battery_charge_total_entity: Cumulative lifetime energy charged into the battery (kWh) — used to auto-detect round-trip efficiency
data_description.battery_discharge_total_entity: Cumulative lifetime energy discharged from the battery (kWh) — used to auto-detect round-trip efficiency

spot_price.title: Electricity Price Entity
spot_price.description: Select an HA sensor that provides the current spot price excluding VAT (DKK/kWh). Works with Strømligning, Tibber, or any compatible integration.

solar_source.title: Solar Forecast Source
solar_source.description: Where should Solar AI get the solar production forecast from? EVCC reads Solcast under the hood; Forecast.Solar is a native HA integration; Auto tries EVCC first and falls back to Forecast.Solar.

data.forecast_solar_entity: Forecast.Solar entity (only used if source is Forecast.Solar or Auto)

data_description.forecast_solar_entity: Typically sensor.energy_production_today — Solar AI reads the per-hour 'watts' attribute

battery.title: Battery & Trading Parameters
battery.description: Configure your battery and the arbitrage thresholds.

data_description.battery_floor_soc: Battery will not export below this state of charge
data_description.battery_max_soc: Battery will not grid-charge above this state of charge
data_description.dso_gln: Select your distribution grid operator. Used to fetch hourly network tariffs from Energi Data Service.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.description: Leave the box ticked to create the Solar AI dashboard automatically (you still need the custom Lovelace cards from HACS — see the README). Existing dashboards: {dashboards}

data.create_dashboard: Create the Solar AI dashboard for me

error.evcc_unreachable: Could not reach EVCC at the specified URL
error.evcc_no_battery: EVCC is reachable but reports no battery
error.entity_not_found: One or more entities could not be found in Home Assistant
error.must_acknowledge_no_ev: You must tick the acknowledgement to continue

abort.no_foxess_modbus: The FoxESS Modbus integration isn't installed yet (or has no entities). Solar AI reads and controls your FoxESS inverter through it, so install and set up FoxESS Modbus first, confirm its sensors are working, then add Solar AI.

init.title: Solar AI — Parameters
init.description: Step 1 of 3 — battery & trading parameters. Press Submit to continue to the OCPP / EV settings on the next page.

data.tariff_fetch_enabled: Fetch Danish DatahubPricelist tariffs (DSO + Energinet + indfødningstarif). Turn off for non-Danish installs.
data.solcast_entity: Solcast forecast — today (sensor with detailedHourly attribute)

buy_price.title: Buy-price source (v0.29.0)
buy_price.description: Choose how the buy price is computed. Manual stack composes the price from VAT + markup + tariffs + elafgift fields. Strømligning fetches the per-15-min all-in price from your chosen Danish retailer via stromligning.dk's public API. The override toggle lets manual VAT/markup/elafgift values take precedence over Strømligning's values while still using its DSO and Energinet tariff data.

data.stromligning_use_manual_overrides: Use manual VAT / markup / elafgift overrides (advanced) — Denmark only
data.octopus_product_code: Octopus product (Agile / Tracker / Cosy / Go / Fixed / etc.) — UK only
data.octopus_region: Octopus GSP region (DNO zone letter A–P) — UK only
data.sell_side_company: Sell-side company (who pays for your solar export)

ocpp_settings.title: OCPP Settings
ocpp_settings.description: Step 2 of 3 — EV charge controller. Solar AI hosts its own embedded OCPP 1.6 server (no separate HACS integration needed). Point your charger's backend URL to ws://<ha-ip>:<port>/<cpid>/. Turn off the embedded server only if you want to use lbbrhzn/ocpp instead.

data.ocpp_embedded: Use Solar AI's embedded OCPP server (recommended)
data.ev_charger_backend: Charger backend (OCPP, or FoxESS Modbus TCP for single-phase solar following)
data.foxess_charger_host: FoxESS charger host (IP) — only for the Modbus backend, e.g. 192.168.x.x
data.ev_ocpp_charge_point_id: Charge point ID (CPID) — used as URL path and entity prefix (e.g. charger, foxess_l11pmc)
data.ev_ocpp_status_entity: Status sensor entity (only used if embedded server is OFF — override only if needed)
data.ev_ocpp_power_entity: Power sensor entity (only used if embedded server is OFF — override only if needed)
data.ev_control_interval_seconds: Control loop interval (seconds, 5–60) — how often the charger is re-evaluated
data.ev_start_window_seconds: Start window (seconds, 10–600) — surplus must hold this long before charging starts
data.ev_stop_window_seconds: Stop window (seconds, 30–1800) — surplus must stay below min this long before charging stops
data.ev_charge_threshold_w: EV charging detection threshold (W, 500–10000) — above this the EV is considered truly charging
data.ocpp_restart_strict: Strict OCPP restart compatibility (spec-compliant chargers only). Off (default) allows RemoteStart from Charging and Finishing in addition to Preparing / SuspendedEV / SuspendedEVSE — required for FoxESS L11PMC.
data.ocpp_remote_start_cooldown_s: RemoteStartTransaction cooldown (seconds, 5–300) — minimum gap between consecutive restart attempts on the same charger

entities.title: Solar AI — Entity Mapping
entities.description: Step 3 of 3 — map Solar AI to your inverter and battery sensors. Change these if your entity IDs differ from the FoxESS Modbus defaults.

reserve_percentile_pct.name: Reserve percentile (%) — forecast-error coverage once the overnight learner is warm (80 = prior fixed behaviour)

ev_battery_priority_soc.name: Battery-first threshold (%) — EV waits until house battery reaches this SoC

solar_confidence_pct.name: Solar confidence (%) — forecast percentile the optimiser plans against (50 = median)

force_export.name: Force export
force_export.description: Immediately activate Force Discharge mode regardless of price conditions.

force_grid_charge.name: Force grid charge
force_grid_charge.description: Immediately activate grid charging via EVCC.

restore_normal.name: Restore normal
restore_normal.description: Cancel active export or grid-charge and restore Self-Use mode.

reset_learning.name: Reset learned data
reset_learning.description: Clear all learned charge rates and load history.

missing_dashboard_cards.title: Solar AI dashboard cards not installed
missing_dashboard_cards.description: The Solar AI dashboard uses custom Lovelace cards that aren't installed yet: {cards}. Install them from HACS → Frontend, then reload your browser (the documentation link below has each card).
